title: Night-sky brightness varies ~4x across the catalog
oneline: Deterministic background-luma normalization hits target exactly with no edge artifacts; regen at <=50 also works
summary: Recomputed bgLuma for all 94 shipped night raws: 16.5-65.3, median 36, with 17 pages over 50. Approach (b) works end-to-end: flood-fill the scoreNightness background region at full res, scale RGB by a single k=target/median with a feathered mask and bright-pixel (white line) protection - cow-wide 65->30.3 and unicorn-wide 62->30.0 with byte-untouched subjects/clouds, crisp lines, and all other gates still passing. Approach (a) also works (cow-wide regen passed at bgLuma 48 with --night-luma-max 50, on attempt 4/4) but costs API calls and replaces the artwork. Approach (c) ordering is defeated by a measured 14.4 mean intra-page tall/wide bgLuma gap (owl alone spans 16.5-54.7) since page order is shared across orientations.
tried: Offline bgLuma recompute of all 94 night raws with the exact scoreNightness machinery (vs their chalks)
Deterministic post-normalization (b) on the 4 brightest pages: full-res flood-filled bg region, multiplicative luma scaling toward target 30, feathered mask + luma-based white-line protection
Edge-artifact hunt: 2x zoom before/after crops at flower/grass/cloud boundaries plus pixel sampling and diff maps
Gate-compatibility check: re-scored normalized raws with scoreNightness and scoreLineColor
Regen (a) of farm/cow-wide with --night-luma-max 50 --max-attempts 4 (4 Gemini calls)
Ordering assessment (c): farm book dusk->midnight order from measured bgLuma, checked per-orientation monotonicity
worked: Normalization hit the target exactly: cow-wide 65.3->30.3, unicorn-wide 61.6->30.0, ant-wide and balloon-wide ~30; no visible edge artifacts, enclosed regions byte-identical, lines stay white
scoreLineColor still passes on all four normalized pages (cow 199->199, ant 249->248, balloon 253->252, unicorn 168->160)
Regen with the gate tightened to <=50 passed on the catalog's brightest page (bgLuma 48, drift 0.0001, lineW 209) within the 4-attempt budget
failed: First normalizer version had blotchy artifacts from a sharp gotcha: blur().raw() on a 1-channel raw buffer silently returns 3 channels (sRGB conversion), so the mask indexed garbage - fixed with .toColourspace('b-w')
Dusk->midnight ordering (c) cannot work per-book: tall/wide variants of the same page differ by 14.4 bgLuma on average (owl 16.5 vs 54.7) and the page order is shared across orientations
limitations: Normalized pages were judged on simulated composites and zoom crops, not a contact-sheet human pass; nothing was committed or punched
Luma-based line protection erodes lineWhite margin slightly on dim-lined pages (unicorn 168->160, still over the 150 bar); distance-based protection is the fallback
A single multiplicative k dims any intentional bright accent in the open background (none present on the four pages tried)
bgLuma conflates sky and border-connected open ground - wides average +10 over talls because they carry more ground
